plan_metadata.name: Sample One Productivity Hub using Toolkit Local Debug

steps.description: Enter the text 'One Productivity Hub using Toolkit' into the search input box located at the top of the 'Samples' pane in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "Create" button associated with the sample titled "One Productivity Hub using Graph Toolkit (Tab, JS, SSO)" in the "Samples" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selection of the default folder path in the "Workspace Folder" dialog box within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion 'Preview README.MD' is visible
@assertion the page title contains 'Getting Started with One Productivity Hub'
Perform a right-click on the tab titled "Preview README.md" located in the upper interface of Visual Studio Code within the active workspace.
Click the "Close" option in the context menu of the file tab within Visual Studio Code to close the currently active file (Preview README.md).
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code while viewing the Explorer panel.
Type the text 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging' into the Visual Studio Code command palette, accessed via the top input field with the '>' prefix.
Press the "Enter" key to select the highlighted command "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" within the Visual Studio Code Command Palette.
Type 'Debug in Teams (Chrome)' into the Command Palette search bar within the Visual Studio Code interface to filter debugging options.
Press "Enter" to select the "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" option in the debugging configuration dropdown within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Sign in" button in the Visual Studio Code Microsoft 365 toolkit prompt to authenticate with a Microsoft 365 account.
Type the email address 'test14@xxbdw.onmicrosoft.com' into the 'Email or phone' input field on the Microsoft Sign-In page.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the entered email address in the Microsoft "Sign in" interface on the login.microsoftonline.com webpage.
Type text: ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the 'Password' field on the Microsoft login page.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the password on the Microsoft login page interface.
Click the "Close" button in the browser tab to exit the "M365 Account - Sign In" confirmation page.
Click inside the "Email, phone, or Skype" input field on the Microsoft sign-in page.
Type text 'test14@xxbdw.onmicrosoft.com' into the 'Email, phone, or Skype' input field on the Microsoft account sign-in page.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the email address entered in the Microsoft sign-in form on the login.microsoftonline.com page.
Type password ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the 'Password' field on the Microsoft login page.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the password and trigger the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login page.
Click the "Yes" button on the "Stay signed in?" dialog of the Microsoft login page.
Click the "Add" button to install the "graph-connector-app" in Microsoft Teams.
Click the "Open" button in the "Added successfully!" dialog box within the Microsoft Teams interface.
Click the “+"  browser tab in Microsoft Teams  to open the new page
Type the URL 'https://localhost:53000/index.html#/tab' into the browser's address bar at the top of the Google Chrome window.
Press the 'Enter' key to navigate to the URL "https://localhost:53000/index.html#/tab" entered in Chrome's address bar.
Click the "Advanced" button on the browser's "Your connection is not private" error page at coordinates (256, 511).
Click on the "Proceed to localhost (unsafe)" link in the browser's "Your connection is not private" warning page.
Click the "One Productivity Hub" browser tab at the top-left corner to activate it.
Click the "Turn on" button in the notification bar at the top of the Microsoft Teams web interface to enable desktop notifications.
Click the "Reload app" option in the dropdown menu within the Microsoft Teams web application interface.
Click the "Allow" button in the popup requesting network device connection access for teams.microsoft.com.
Click the "Start One Productivity Hub" button in the Microsoft Teams app interface to initiate the application setup.
Click the "Accept" button to grant permissions requested for the application "one-productivity-hub-aad" on the Microsoft login consent page.
@assertion 'Calendar events', 'To-do tasks', 'Files' are visible
